title: Albert Aparicio Garcia
text:
Research Engineer — Atmospheric Composition Group
Barcelona Supercomputing Center (BSC-CNS)
📍 Barcelona, Catalonia
I am a physicist and meteorologist working at the Atmospheric Composition group of the Barcelona Supercomputing Center. My work focuses on emission data processing, pollutant emission estimation methodologies, and modelling. Previously, I worked as a Climate Services Technician at the Meteorological Service of Catalonia (SMC), where I developed tools for climate monitoring, data quality control, and user-facing meteorological reports.

{{< fa envelope >}} Email {{< fa brands linkedin >}} LinkedIn

title: Albert Aparicio Garcia
text:
Research Interests

Atmospheric composition — emission inventories, pollutant dispersion modelling
Climate services — climate monitoring, degree-day databases, extreme event reporting
Numerical modelling — WRF, AERMOD, CALPUFF, air quality modelling
Data science — Python, R, GIS (ArcGIS, QGIS), SQL, climate data analysis

title: Awards & Recognition
text:
Research & Science Communication Awards

🏆 Outstanding Poster Award — EMS Annual Meeting 2024
European Meteorological Society
📅 2024
Awarded unanimously by the committee for the poster “Enhancing meteorological report access and response time: How the Meteorological Service of Catalonia addresses the rise in weather-related claims”.
{{< fa link >}} Award announcement

Departament d’Economia i Coneixement, Generalitat de Catalunya
📅 2011
Award for young researchers. Recognised work: “Testing the energy of the future: Dye Solar Cells”.

📅 2011
Honourable mention for the work: “Testing the energy of the future: Dye Solar Cells”.

Award promoting scientific spirit among young people. Recognised work: “Palafrugell, un viatge pel seu clima”.

title: Awards & Recognition
text:
Fellowships & Programmes

Programa Joves i Ciència
Fundació Catalunya — La Pedrera
📅 2009–2011
Selected as a participant in this competitive programme providing young students with their first research experience. Developed a project on organic photovoltaic energy.
